item.name: Returns all appealable issues of the selected appeal type for a claimant.

request.name: Returns all appealable issues of the selected appeal type for a claimant.

description.content: Returns all issues of the selected appeal type associated with a claimant that have been decided as of the `receiptDate`. Not all issues returned are guaranteed to be eligible for appeal.

query.description: Type of benefit. Required if decisionReviewType is higher-level-reviews or supplemental-claims. Ignored if decisionReviewType is notice-of-disagreements.

(Required) (yyyy-mm-dd) Cutoff date for requested issues. The API returns issues that have been decided as of this date. This date must be after 2019-02-19, the Appeals Modernization Act (AMA) activation date.
Claimant's Integration Control Number (ICN). Optional when using a veteran-scoped token. Required when using a representative- or system-scoped token.

response.name: Claimant's appealable issues matching request parameters

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

The Appealable Issues API lets you retrieve a claimant's previously decided (appealable) issues and any chains of preceding issues. An "issue" is a problem listed in a claim to VA, and a claim may include multiple issues. Claimants may be Veterans or dependents of Veterans, such as spouses, children (biological and step), and parents (biological and foster).

You can use appealable issues data to submit a Higher-Level Review, Notice of Disagreement, or Supplemental Claim. Note that issues returned by the API may be eligible for appeal to VA, but eligibility is not guaranteed.

To check the status of all decision reviews or appeals for a specified individual, use the [Appeals Status API](/explore/api/appeals-status/docs).

## Technical overview

### Authentication and authorization
The authorization model for the Appealable Issues API uses OAuth 2.0/OpenID Connect. The following models are supported:
* [Authorization Code Grant (ACG)](/explore/api/appealable-issues/authorization-code)
* [Client Credentials Grant (CCG)](/explore/api/appealable-issues/client-credentials)

**Important:** To get production access using client credentials grant, you must either work for VA or have specific VA agreements in place. If you have questions, [contact us](/support/contact-us).

### Test data
Our sandbox environment is populated with [claimant test data](/explore/api/appealable-issues/test-users) that can be used to test various response scenarios. This sandbox data contains no PII or PHI, but mimics real claimant account information.


Contact Support:
 Name: developer.va.gov
